connections.label: input source positive node to resistor input
resistor output to RC output node
capacitor top plate connected to output node
capacitor bottom plate connected to ground

sources.label: ECG-like input with 60 Hz interference and optional high-frequency noise

requested_outputs.label: Compute RC cutoff frequency from R1 and C1
Compute attenuation at Nyquist frequency for fs = 500 Hz
Explain whether a single-pole RC is sufficient for anti-aliasing
Identify Vout probe location in Simscape/Simulink
Diagnose accidental use of 100 uF instead of 100 nF

likely_analysis: first-order RC low-pass frequency response, sampling/Nyquist anti-aliasing discussion, and Simscape transient/frequency-domain verification using Vout relative to ground
assumptions: The ADC input is treated as high impedance unless a specific input impedance is later supplied.
The output voltage Vout is the node between R1 and C1 measured with respect to ground.
The sample output block represents the ADC/sample stream and is not part of the passive RC electrical network.
The input source may be modeled as a sum of sinusoidal components plus optional noise for simulation.
The prompt requests calculations and explanation, but numerical authority should come from verified formulas, MATLAB calculations, or the generated model rather than LLM-only answers.
Live correction-pass instruction requests a Simscape-first SATK/Codex build, visible model opening, and stopping before screenshots for manual diagram arrangement.
ambiguities: The exact ECG waveform amplitude, 60 Hz amplitude, high-frequency noise spectrum, and source impedance are not specified.
ADC resolution, quantization, input impedance, sample-and-hold behavior, and anti-aliasing requirements are not specified.
The image shows a functional ADC and samples block, but not detailed ADC internals.
suggested_simscape_blocks: Simscape Electrical Controlled Voltage Source or AC Voltage Source
Simulink Sine Wave blocks for 5 Hz and 60 Hz components
Simulink Band-Limited White Noise or noise source for optional high-frequency noise
Simulink Zero-Order Hold or ADC-equivalent sampling block with sample rate 500 Hz
Simulink Scope or To Workspace for Vout and samples

focus_points.label: Vout probe node
focus_points.reason: This is the filter output and ADC input, so it is the required voltage probe location.
focus_points.teaching_question: Why is the voltage across C1, rather than the voltage before R1, the low-pass filter output?

focus_points.label: R1-C1 cutoff setting
focus_points.reason: R1 and C1 set the filter time constant and cutoff frequency.
focus_points.teaching_question: How do R and C together determine where the low-pass response starts attenuating?

focus_points.label: ADC sampling at 500 Hz
focus_points.reason: The ADC sampling frequency defines the Nyquist frequency for the anti-aliasing discussion.
focus_points.teaching_question: What frequency boundary does a 500 Hz sampler create for signals entering the ADC?

focus_points.label: 100 uF instead of 100 nF
focus_points.reason: A capacitance unit mistake changes the RC time constant and cutoff frequency by a large factor.
focus_points.teaching_question: If the capacitor value becomes much larger, what happens to the cutoff frequency and the ECG signal?

focus_points.label: Probe Vout
focus_points.reason: The diagram explicitly marks the output probe at the resistor-capacitor junction.
focus_points.teaching_question: Where would you connect a voltage sensor to measure the ADC input voltage?

focus_points.label: Single-pole anti-aliasing limit
focus_points.reason: The task asks whether one RC pole is enough to guarantee alias-free sampling.
focus_points.teaching_question: What does a first-order filter still allow above Nyquist, even after attenuation?

focus_points.label: Capacitor unit check
focus_points.reason: The specified value is 100 nF, while the lab mistake is 100 uF.
focus_points.teaching_question: How many times larger is 100 uF than 100 nF, and why does that matter for this filter?
